questions: An insured travelling abroad (Imperial Plus plan, excluding USA) has a medical emergency in the USA during a 3-week business trip. Is the treatment covered?

answers.answer: The policy covers emergency treatment outside the area of cover for up to six weeks per trip within the Sum Insured limit, including treatment required due to an Accident or the sudden beginning or worsening of a severe Illness which presents an immediate threat to Your health. However, there is a waiting period of 30 days for out-patient claims under international cover, except those arising out of Accidental Injury. The policy also covers medical repatriation to the home country for treatment, if necessary, and the cost of return trip to the country from where the insured was repatriated within one month after treatment.
answers.justification: Section D) Exclusions- Standard Exclusions Applicable to Part B- International Cover, Section C) Benefits Covered Under the Policy, and Section 13) Medical Repatriation

relevant_clauses.section: Section D) Exclusions- Standard Exclusions Applicable to Part B- International Cover
relevant_clauses.content: We will pay the Customary and Reasonable expenses, up to the limit specified in the Policy Schedule, incurred for treatment of medical emergencies which occur during business or holiday trips outside Your area of cover. Cover is provided for up to six weeks per trip within the Sum Insured limit.

relevant_clauses.section: Section C) Benefits Covered Under the Policy
relevant_clauses.content: During the first year of Global Health Care Policy with Us, 30 days waiting period would be applicable for all claims under out-patient except those arising out of Accidental Injury, however the waiting period would not be applied during subsequent renewals.

If the necessary treatment for which You are covered isn't available locally You can choose to be medically evacuated to Your home country for treatment, instead of to the nearest appropriate medical centre. This only applies when Your home country is within Your geographical area of cover.

reasoning_trace.thought: The policy name is Imperial Plus, but the insured is in the USA, which is excluded from the policy.
